skill.name: pdf
skill.description: Comprehensive PDF toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating PDFs, merging or splitting files, and filling PDF forms with local Python and command-line tools.
skill.summary: Process, generate, merge, split, inspect, and fill PDFs using local tooling.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic skill with local PDF processing guidance and helper scripts. Static findings are expected for a PDF toolkit because it reads and writes user-selected files and documents optional command-line tools; no malicious behavior or prompt injection was found.

content.value_statement: PDF work often requires precise extraction, conversion, merging, and form handling. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical workflows for local PDF automation.
actual_capabilities: Extract text, metadata, and tables from PDF files with Python libraries.
Merge, split, rotate, watermark, optimize, and repair PDF documents.
Create new PDFs with text, pages, tables, and simple layouts.
Detect fillable form fields and write validated field values to PDFs.
Add text annotations to non-fillable forms after visual bounding box validation.
Convert PDF pages to images for OCR, inspection, and form placement checks.
limitations: Scanned PDFs require OCR tools and may need manual quality review.
Encrypted PDFs require the correct password and compatible local tools.
Complex layouts can need manual inspection before reliable extraction.
Form annotation appearance can vary across PDF viewers.

use_cases.title: Extract Contract Tables
use_cases.description: Pull clauses, tables, and metadata from agreements for review or spreadsheet analysis.

use_cases.title: Prepare Document Packets
use_cases.description: Merge, split, rotate, watermark, and optimize PDFs for client or internal delivery.

use_cases.title: Fill Application Forms
use_cases.description: Identify form fields, validate entries, and create filled PDFs with repeatable local scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Extract PDF Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a single readable PDF.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to extract text from my PDF. Preserve page breaks and summarize any pages with missing text.

prompt_templates.title: Merge and Reorder Documents
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine several PDFs into one organized packet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to merge these PDFs in the order I provide. Rotate pages when needed and create one final document.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Tables to Spreadsheet
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn PDF tables into structured spreadsheet content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to extract all tables from this PDF. Put the results into a spreadsheet and note uncertain rows.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a Non-Fillable Form
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced form workflow that needs visual validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF form workflow to fill this non-fillable PDF. Create validation images, inspect bounding boxes, then generate the completed PDF.

output_examples.input: A 24-page vendor PDF with several invoice tables.
output_examples.output: A spreadsheet with extracted invoice rows, plus a short note listing pages where table borders caused uncertainty.

output_examples.input: Three separate PDF packets that need ordering, rotation, and one watermark.
output_examples.output: One merged PDF with corrected page orientation, a consistent watermark, and the original files left unchanged.

output_examples.input: A benefits enrollment form without fillable fields.
output_examples.output: A completed PDF with text annotations placed in validated entry areas and checkboxes marked in the correct squares.

best_practices: Work on copies of original PDFs before editing or form filling.
Inspect rendered page images when extraction, OCR, or annotation placement is uncertain.
Validate form field IDs, checkbox values, and bounding boxes before generating final documents.
anti_patterns: Do not assume text extraction is complete for scanned or image-heavy PDFs.
Do not place annotations without checking that entry boxes avoid label text.
Do not remove encryption or passwords unless you are authorized to access the document.

faq.question: Can this skill edit existing PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports common edits such as merging, splitting, rotating, watermarking, form filling, and adding annotations.

faq.question: Can it extract tables from PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends pdfplumber and spreadsheet workflows, but complex table layouts may need manual review.

faq.question: Does it work with scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, when OCR tools are available. OCR quality depends on scan resolution, page clarity, and language support.

faq.question: Can it fill PDF forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It handles fillable fields directly and uses validated text annotations for non-fillable forms.

faq.question: Does it send documents to external services?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflows use local libraries and command-line tools unless the user adds outside services.

faq.question: What tools does it rely on?
faq.answer: It uses Python libraries such as pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab, and optional tools such as qpdf and poppler utilities.
